author: mayor
text: Research epic community thread started. This epic tracks the dashboard & bead structure audit for convoy hq-dezt. Findings: 5/9 statuses unmapped, no priority colors, ready_issues view unused, gates not persistent, progress gauge sentinel bug, cross-bead deps incomplete, nodeGraph gate viz missing. Dashboard fixes in progress: auto-discovered DB dropdowns, consistent id+title dropdowns, convoy/epic metadata on all 25 beads across 4 rigs.

author: lora_forge/crew/forge
text:
## Implementation Complete

**Commit:** 8655ffa9 on `integration/crew-nvim-otel` (sfgastown)
**Also:** 8437259 on `main` (lora_forge — actual .envrc instance)
**Files:** `scripts/crew-envrc-template.sh` (template), `crew/forge/.envrc` (instance)

### What was done
- Created direnv `.envrc` template for crew directories
- Sets GT_ROLE, GT_RIG, GT_CREW, GT_AGENT, BD_ACTOR, BEADS_AGENT_NAME, GIT_AUTHOR_NAME
- GT_AGENT=claudecode-nvim distinguishes neovim-embedded from CLI sessions
- OTel propagation when GT_OTEL_METRICS_URL is set
- Deployed to lora_forge/crew/forge/ and verified via `direnv export bash`

### Design Decision
Used direnv `.envrc` rather than Go code change because crew sessions launched from neovim bypass `gt crew start` entirely. The shell hook is the only injection point before Claude Code starts.

### Verification
```
$ eval "$(direnv export bash)" && env | grep GT_
GT_AGENT=claudecode-nvim
GT_CREW=forge
GT_RIG=lora_forge
GT_ROLE=lora_forge/crew/forge
GT_ROOT=/home/ubuntu/gt
```

author: lora_forge/crew/forge
text:
## No Code Changes Needed

**Why:** subprocess.go L28-32 already reads GT_AGENT and maps it to gt.harness in OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES. With sg-nf0o setting GT_AGENT=claudecode-nvim via .envrc, the harness type automatically appears as gt.harness=claudecode-nvim on all OTel spans.

**Verification:** Simulated buildGTResourceAttrs output:
```
OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES=gt.role=lora_forge/crew/forge,gt.rig=lora_forge,gt.agent=forge,gt.harness=claudecode-nvim
```

No changes to subprocess.go, env.go, or any Go code required.

author: lora_forge/crew/forge
text:
## Implementation Complete

**Commit:** 107b5a09 on `integration/crew-nvim-otel`
**Files:** `opentelemetry/grafana/provisioning/dashboards/gastown-overview.json`, `lora-forge-sessions.json`

### What was done
- Added `harness` template variable (dropdown) to both dashboards
- Query: `label_values(gastown_session_starts_total, harness)`
- gastown-overview: 1 panel query updated with `harness=~"$harness"` filter
- lora-forge-sessions: 9 panel queries updated, new harness dropdown added
- Both include "All" default so existing behavior unchanged

### Pattern followed
Same structure as existing `role` template variable — multi-select, includeAll, refresh on time range change.

author: lora_forge/crew/forge
text:
## Epic Complete: Crew/Neovim Runtime OTel Integration

All 3 tasks closed. Branch: `integration/crew-nvim-otel` (2 commits).

### Summary

**sg-nf0o** (8655ffa9): Crew env injection
- direnv `.envrc` template sets GT_ROLE, GT_RIG, GT_CREW, GT_AGENT=claudecode-nvim
- Deployed instance to lora_forge/crew/forge/
- OTel propagation included when GT_OTEL_METRICS_URL is set

**sg-7e79**: Harness type registration
- No code changes needed — subprocess.go already maps GT_AGENT → gt.harness
- With .envrc setting GT_AGENT=claudecode-nvim, spans automatically get gt.harness=claudecode-nvim

**sg-a5j1** (107b5a09): Grafana dashboard filters
- gastown-overview: harness dropdown + 1 panel filter
- lora-forge-sessions: harness dropdown + 9 panel filters

### End-to-end flow
.envrc → GT_AGENT=claudecode-nvim → subprocess.go → gt.harness=claudecode-nvim → OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES → Grafana filter

### Pending
- Integration branch needs merge to main via refinery
- Close convoy hq-cv-aoc
